State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662            322-5660           322-5901          322-6441


                                                June 7, 1984




Honorable Henry J. Mello
Senator, 17th District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA   95814
                                                Re:  Advice Letter No. A-84-137

Dear Senator Mello:

     This is in reply to your letter of May 17, 1984, concerning
the advice I provided to Ms. Eva Nelson regarding a mailing sent
by your office to local governmental entities located within your
senatorial district.

     The advice provided to Ms. Nelson, as stated in your letter,
is correct.  For your information, enclosed is a copy of 2 Cal.
Admin. Code Section 18901.  Section (d)(2) addresses your
specific question.

     If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

                                                Sincerely,

                                                [signature]
                                                Mary Ann Kvasager
                                                Political Reform Consultant

MAK:bb
Enclosure

Senate FPPC
California Legislature MAY 24 12 11 PM '84

HENRY J. MELLO
SEVENTEENTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT

Senate Majority Whip

May 17, 1984


Ms. Mary Ann Kvasacer
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Kvasacer:

This letter is a request for a written response, in addition to the oral
response you gave to my staff person, Eva Nelson, on May 16 regarding a
mailing to local governmental entities in my district.

The question was whether a 250-piece mailing to cities, counties, special
districts, school districts and community colleges in the 17th Senate District
transmitting budget material was exempt from the 200-piece limit.

Your response was that mailings in the normal course of business from one
governmental entity to another governmental entity are exempt from the
200-piece limitation.

I would appreciate receiving this response in writing.

Sincerely,

[signature]
HENRY J. MELLO

HJM:EN:wj

STATE CAPITOL                  1200 AGUAJITO ROAD          701 OCEAN STREET              240 CHURCH STREET         7365-G MONTEREY STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814          MONTEREY, CA 93940           SANTA CRUZ, CA 95060          SALINAS, CA 93901         GILROY, CA 95020
(916) 445-5843                (408) 373-0773               (408) 425-0401                (408) 757-4169            (408) 848-1437

The letter is dated May 17, 1984, and requests a written response regarding whether a 250-piece mailing to local governmental entities in the senator's district was exempt from the 200-piece limit.
